refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs focusing on one aspect each. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis without implying any implementation or combining multiple requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide data or models specifically representing the terrain characteristics of the worksite, independent of obstacles or work-zone details.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of worksite obstacles.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital model representing the spatial features of the worksite terrain
Extract and classify key terrain features relevant to planning and safety
Provide an interface to share the terrain model with planning and execution modules
Continuously update the terrain model based on new data or changes
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital model representing the spatial features of the worksite terrain

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP encapsulates the core function of creating a terrain model, which can be independently developed and then interfaced with planning and execution functions. This supports modularity and reduces coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that the model may not capture all safety-relevant features if not properly specified
Assumes that planning and execution modules can consume the model format provided
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for updating or maintaining the terrain model to handle dynamic changes, ensuring the model remains current and reliable for safety and operational planning.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital models representing spatial features of the worksite terrain, including data acquisition, modeling algorithms, and data output.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and spatial measurements from the worksite.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed digital terrain models to other modules for task planning and execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and remote modules for model updates and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data outputs are well defined.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control; terrain modeling would require extensive data sharing and cause diffusion.
Partial containment possible but teleoperation focuses on remote control, not terrain modeling; would cause diffusion of modeling logic.
Very low containment; safety services focus on alerts and monitoring, not terrain modeling; would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the digital terrain modeling DP because it naturally encompasses spatial data processing and model generation, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality.
Integration challenges with other modules consuming the terrain model data.
Risk of NGHE Solution becoming a bottleneck if terrain modeling is computationally intensive.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital model representing the spatial features of the worksite terrain' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying the creation of a digital spatial model, which is a clear and verifiable functional solution.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk identified as the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module with a low diffusion score (2).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the terrain modeling DP, with well-defined interfaces for data input (Telemetry) and output (Tasking), minimizing diffusion and ensuring clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces the required digital terrain model accessible to planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, with minimal overlap or diffusion into other modules.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate risks related to dependency on real-time telemetry data quality and availability.
Plan for integration testing with modules consuming terrain model data to ensure smooth data exchange.
Assess computational load to prevent NGHE Solution module from becoming a bottleneck and consider scalability or parallelization if needed.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear satisfaction of requirements, minimal diffusion risk, and feasible verification. However, given the high safety criticality and the complexity of terrain analysis, further decomposition into sub-FRs is warranted to ensure detailed coverage of all aspects such as data acquisition, model generation, and data dissemination. This will help in managing risks related to telemetry data quality, integration, and computational load as recommended in the revisions.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data of the worksite terrain
Process telemetry data to generate accurate digital terrain model
Provide terrain model data to planning and execution modules

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is capable of handling the computational load with potential scalability.
Telemetry data quality and availability can be monitored and managed effectively.
open_risks: Dependency on real-time telemetry data quality and availability.
Potential computational bottlenecks in terrain model generation.
Integration risks with modules consuming terrain model data.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming accurate and timely terrain model generation.
Integration test results with planning and execution modules.
Performance metrics demonstrating computational efficiency and scalability.
